--help.text:
Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Main entry point for the chemsmart CLI.

Options:
  --version                 Show the version and exit.
  --verbose / --no-verbose
  --help                    Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  agent   Run the chemsmart agent.
  config  Configure chemsmart.
  run     Run chemsmart jobs.
  sub     Submit chemsmart jobs.
  update  Update chemsmart metadata.

agent --help.text:
Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main agent [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  AI-scientist agent commands for chemsmart.

Options:
  --plain        Run the agent TUI in plain inline mode for conservative
                 terminals.
  -v, --verbose  Keep console logging at DEBUG instead of writing it only to
                 the agent log file.
  --help         Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  ask             Run a one-shot dry-run request and stream Rich output.
  doctor          Validate api.env, AI_PROVIDER, and provider connectivity.
  resume          Resume an agent session.
  run             Plan and execute an agent workflow.
  sessions        List saved agent sessions.
  tools           List registered agent tools.
  wizard          Probe a target server and render or write a wizard YAML
                  config.
  wizard-refresh  Refresh or reuse the wizard node sidecar cache for a server.
  wizard-verify   Verify wizard/server transport wiring for an existing YAML.

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Main command for running chemsmart jobs.

  This command sets up the job runner with specified computational resources
  and executes jobs directly in the current environment.

Options:
  -s, --server TEXT               Server name. If not specified, will try to
                                  automatically determine and use the current
                                  server.
  -n, --num-cores INTEGER         Number of cores for each job.
  -g, --num-gpus INTEGER          Number of GPUs per node. Defaults to number
                                  of GPUs on the specified server if None.
  -m, --mem-gb INTEGER            Memory in GB.
  --fake / --no-fake              If true, fake job runners will be used.
  --scratch / --no-scratch        Run in scratch mode or without a scratch
                                  folder.
  --delete-scratch / --no-delete-scratch
                                  If job was run in scratch, delete the
                                  scratch folder after the job is completed
                                  successfully.
  --debug / --no-debug            Turn on debug logging.
  --stream / --no-stream          Turn on logging to stdout.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  database         CLI for chemsmart database operations.
  gaussian         CLI subcommand for running Gaussian jobs using the...
  grouper          Group/cluster molecular structures based on various...
  iterate          CLI subcommand for running iterate jobs using the...
  mol              CLI subcommand for running PyMOL visualization jobs...
  nciplot          CLI subcommand for running NCIPLOT jobs using the...
  orca             Main CLI command group for running ORCA jobs using the...
  thermochemistry  CLI subcommand for running thermochemistry jobs using...

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run gaussian [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  CLI subcommand for running Gaussian jobs using the chemsmart framework.

Options:
  -p, --project TEXT              Project settings.
  -f, --filename TEXT             Filename from which new input is prepared.
  -l, --label TEXT                Write user input filename for the job
                                  (without extension). Special characters such
                                  as apostrophe, comma, asterisk etc should be
                                  avoided.
  -a, --append-label TEXT         Name to be appended to file for the job.
                                  Special characters such as apostrophe,
                                  comma, asterisk etc should be avoided.
  -i, --index TEXT                Index of molecules to use; 1-based indices.
                                  If not specified, all molecules are passed
                                  to the job. Jobs that need only one molecule
                                  will use the last one. For chemsmart
                                  database files (.db), use together with
                                  --ri/--rid to select a structure index
                                  within the selected record.
  --ri, --record-index INTEGER    Record index (1-based) within a chemsmart
                                  database.
  --rid, --record-id TEXT         Record ID (or unique prefix) within a
                                  chemsmart database.
  --si, --structure-index TEXT    Structure index (1-based) within the
                                  selected record.
  --sid, --structure-id TEXT      Global structure ID (or unique prefix)
                                  within a chemsmart database.
  --mid, --molecule-id TEXT       Molecule ID (or unique prefix); selects
                                  every conformer of that molecule.
  -t, --title TEXT                Gaussian job title.
  -c, --charge INTEGER            Charge of the molecule.
  -m, --multiplicity INTEGER      Multiplicity of the molecule.
  -x, --functional TEXT           New functional to run.
  -b, --basis TEXT                New basis set to run.
  -s, --semiempirical TEXT        Semiempirical method to run.
  -o, --additional-opt-options TEXT
                                  Additional optimization options.
  -r, --additional-route-parameters TEXT
                                  Extra keywords appended to the Gaussian
                                  route line. There is no separate frequency
                                  subcommand or flag: to run an optimization +
                                  frequency job, pass 'freq' here (adds '
                                  freq' to the route). Example: --additional-
                                  route-parameters freq.
  -A, --append-additional-info TEXT
                                  Additional information to be appended at the
                                  end of the input file (e.g., scrf=read).
  -C, --custom-solvent TEXT       Custom solvent information to be appended at
                                  the end of the input file (e.g., scrf=read).
  -d, --dieze-tag TEXT            Dieze tag for Gaussian job. Possible options
                                  include "n", "p", "t" to get "#n", "#p",
                                  "#t", respectively.
  --forces / --no-forces          Whether to calculate forces.
  --remove-solvent / --no-remove-solvent
                                  Remove the solvent model from the job
                                  (overrides project settings).
  -sm, --solvent-model TEXT       Solvent model to use (e.g. smd, cpcm,
                                  iefpcm).
  -si, --solvent-id TEXT          Solvent identifier (e.g. water, toluene,
                                  dichloromethane).
  -so, --solvent-options TEXT     Additional options appended inside the
                                  scrf=() route keyword (e.g. 'iterative'
                                  gives scrf=(smd,solvent=water,iterative)).
  -P, --pubchem TEXT              Queries structure from PubChem using name,
                                  smiles, cid and conformer information.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  com      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian input file as is.
  crest    CLI subcommand for running Gaussian CREST jobs.
  dias     CLI subcommand for running Gaussian DI-AS jobs.
  irc      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian IRC jobs.
  link     CLI subcommand for running Gaussian link jobs.
  modred   CLI subcommand for running Gaussian modred jobs.
  nci      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian NCI jobs.
  opt      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian optimization calculation.
  qrc      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian QRC jobs.
  resp     CLI subcommand for running Gaussian RESP jobs.
  scan     CLI subcommand for running Gaussian scan jobs.
  sp       CLI subcommand for running Gaussian single point calculation.
  td       CLI subcommand for running Gaussian TDDFT jobs.
  traj     CLI subcommand for running Gaussian set jobs.
  ts       CLI subcommand for running Gaussian transition state calculation.
  userjob  CLI subcommand for running Gaussian custom jobs.
  wbi      CLI subcommand for running Gaussian WBI jobs.

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run gaussian crest [OPTIONS]

  CLI subcommand for running Gaussian CREST jobs.

Options:
  -S, --skip-completed / -R, --no-skip-completed
                                  Skip completed jobs. Use -R to rerun
                                  completed jobs.
  -j, --jobtype TEXT              Gaussian job type. Options: ["opt", "ts",
                                  "modred", "scan", "sp", "irc"].
  -c, --coordinates TEXT          List of coordinates to be fixed for modred
                                  or scan jobs. 1-indexed.
  -s, --step-size TEXT            Step size for coordinate scans.
  -n, --num-steps TEXT            Number of steps for coordinate scans.
  -ih, --ignore-hydrogens         Whether to ignore hydrogens in grouping.
  -np, --num-procs INTEGER        Number of processors to use for the grouper.
  -T, --threshold FLOAT           Threshold for grouping. If not specified,
                                  uses strategy-specific defaults:
                                  RMSD/HRMSD/SpyRMSD/PyMOLRMSD=0.5,
                                  iRMSD=0.125, TFD=0.1, Tanimoto=0.9,
                                  Energy=1.0 (kcal/mol)
  -N, --num-groups INTEGER        Target number of groups to return. This uses
                                  adaptive threshold finding to return
                                  approximately this many unique structures.
  -m, --matrix-format [xlsx|csv|txt]
                                  Output file format for results. Default is
                                  xlsx.
  -E, --energy-type [E|H|G|qhH|qhG|sp_qhG]
                                  Energy type to use for every grouping.
                                  Options: E (SCF energy, default), H
                                  (enthalpy), G (Gibbs free energy), qhH
                                  (quasi-harmonic enthalpy), qhG (quasi-
                                  harmonic Gibbs free energy), sp_qhG (single-
                                  point corrected quasi-harmonic Gibbs free
                                  energy).Note: not for xyz file. When using
                                  qhH, qhG and sp_qhG, chemsmart will call
                                  thermochemistry for thermo correction, Users
                                  can specify the thermo-correction parameter.
  -g, --grouping-strategy [rmsd|hrmsd|spyrmsd|irmsd|pymolrmsd|tanimoto|torsion|isomorphism|formula|connectivity|energy]
                                  Grouping strategy to use for conformer
                                  grouping.
  --inversion [auto|on|off]       Control coordinate inversion checking in
                                  iRMSD grouper: 'auto' (default):
                                  automatically detect, 'on': force check,
                                  'off': disable.
  -ft, --fingerprint-type [rdkit|rdk|morgan|maccs|atompair|torsion|usr|usrcat]
                                  Fingerprint type for tanimoto grouping.
  --use-weights / --no-use-weights
                                  Whether to use torsion weights in TFD
                                  calculation for torsion grouping.
  --max-dev [equal|spec]          Normalization method for TFD: 'equal'
                                  (default) or 'spec'.
  -nc, --num-confs-to-run INTEGER
                                  Number of lowest-energy conformers to submit
                                  for calculation. If not specified, all
                                  conformers will be submitted.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run gaussian qrc [OPTIONS] COMMAND
                                                     [ARGS]...

  CLI subcommand for running Gaussian QRC jobs.

  Examples:     `chemsmart sub gaussian -p proj -f     ts.log qrc` runs QRC
  optimization jobs     after displacing along vibrational mode 1
  (default) by +/- 0.5 Angstroms (default).

      `chemsmart sub gaussian -p proj -f ts.log     qrc -m 2 -a 1.2` runs QRC
      optimization jobs     after displacing along vibrational mode     2 (-m
      2) by +/- 1.2 Angstroms (-a 1.2).

      `chemsmart sub gaussian -p proj -f ts.log     qrc -j ts -m 2 -a 1.5`
      runs QRC TS jobs     after displacing along vibrational mode     2 (-m
      2) by +/- 1.5 Angstroms (-a 1.5).

      `chemsmart sub gaussian -p proj -f ts.log     qrc -j modred -c [1,2] -m
      2 -a 1.5`     runs QRC modred jobs after displacing along
      vibrational mode 2 by +/- 1.5 Angstroms.

Options:
  -S, --skip-completed / -R, --no-skip-completed
                                  Skip completed jobs. Use -R to rerun
                                  completed jobs.
  -j, --jobtype TEXT              Gaussian job type. Options: ["opt", "ts",
                                  "modred", "scan", "sp", "irc"].
  -c, --coordinates TEXT          List of coordinates to be fixed for modred
                                  or scan jobs. 1-indexed.
  -s, --step-size TEXT            Step size for coordinate scans.
  -n, --num-steps TEXT            Number of steps for coordinate scans.
  -m, --mode-idx INTEGER          Mode number of vibrational mode. 1-indexed.
                                  Defaults to 1 (first vibrational mode).
  -a, --amp FLOAT                 Amplitude for displacement for the chosen
                                  vibrational mode. Defaults to 0.5 Angstroms.
  -N, --nframes INTEGER           Number of frames to create molecules along
                                  the vibrational mode. Defaults to None, for
                                  which only one molecule will be created.
  -p, --phase FLOAT               Phase angle (radians) for displacement.
                                  Defaults to pi/2 so that sin(phase) = 1.
  --normalize / --no-normalize    If True, scale the (un-weighted) mode so its
                                  largest per-atom displacement is 1.0, making
                                  `amp` the max displacement. Defaults to
                                  False.
  --return-xyz / --no-return-xyz  If True and `nframes` is set, return a
                                  multi-frame XYZ string.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  qmmm  Run a QM/MM calculation for this job type.

  -S, --skip-completed / -R, --no-skip-completed

  -j, --jobtype TEXT              Gaussian job type. Options: ["opt", "ts",
                                  "modred", "scan", "sp", "irc"].
  -c, --coordinates TEXT          List of coordinates to be fixed for modred
                                  or scan jobs. 1-indexed.
  -s, --step-size TEXT            Step size for coordinate scans.
  -n, --num-steps TEXT            Number of steps for coordinate scans.
  -ih, --ignore-hydrogens         Whether to ignore hydrogens in grouping.
  -np, --num-procs INTEGER        Number of processors to use for the grouper.
  -T, --threshold FLOAT           Threshold for grouping. If not specified,
                                  uses strategy-specific defaults:
                                  RMSD/HRMSD/SpyRMSD/PyMOLRMSD=0.5,
                                  iRMSD=0.125, TFD=0.1, Tanimoto=0.9,
                                  Energy=1.0 (kcal/mol)
  -N, --num-groups INTEGER        Target number of groups to return. This uses
                                  adaptive threshold finding to return
                                  approximately this many unique structures.
  -m, --matrix-format [xlsx|csv|txt]
                                  Output file format for results. Default is
                                  xlsx.
  -E, --energy-type [E|H|G|qhH|qhG|sp_qhG]
                                  Energy type to use for every grouping.
                                  Options: E (SCF energy, default), H
                                  (enthalpy), G (Gibbs free energy), qhH
                                  (quasi-harmonic enthalpy), qhG (quasi-
                                  harmonic Gibbs free energy), sp_qhG (single-
                                  point corrected quasi-harmonic Gibbs free
                                  energy).Note: not for xyz file. When using
                                  qhH, qhG and sp_qhG, chemsmart will call
                                  thermochemistry for thermo correction, Users
                                  can specify the thermo-correction parameter.

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run orca [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Main CLI command group for running ORCA jobs using the chemsmart framework.

  This function serves as the primary entry point for all ORCA quantum
  chemistry calculations. It processes command-line arguments, configures job
  settings, loads molecular structures, and prepares the context for
  subcommands.

Options:
  -p, --project TEXT              Project settings.
  -f, --filename TEXT             Filename from which new input is prepared.
  -l, --label TEXT                Write user input filename for the job
                                  (without extension). Special characters such
                                  as apostrophe, comma, asterisk etc should be
                                  avoided.
  -a, --append-label TEXT         Name to be appended to file for the job.
                                  Special characters such as apostrophe,
                                  comma, asterisk etc should be avoided.
  -i, --index TEXT                Index of molecules to use; 1-based indices.
                                  If not specified, all molecules are passed
                                  to the job. Jobs that need only one molecule
                                  will use the last one. For chemsmart
                                  database files (.db), use together with
                                  --ri/--rid to select a structure index
                                  within the selected record.
  --ri, --record-index INTEGER    Record index (1-based) within a chemsmart
                                  database.
  --rid, --record-id TEXT         Record ID (or unique prefix) within a
                                  chemsmart database.
  --si, --structure-index TEXT    Structure index (1-based) within the
                                  selected record.
  --sid, --structure-id TEXT      Global structure ID (or unique prefix)
                                  within a chemsmart database.
  --mid, --molecule-id TEXT       Molecule ID (or unique prefix); selects
                                  every conformer of that molecule.
  -t, --title TEXT                ORCA job title.
  -c, --charge INTEGER            Charge of the molecule.
  -m, --multiplicity INTEGER      Multiplicity of the molecule.
  -A, --ab-initio TEXT            Ab initio method to be used.
  -x, --functional TEXT           New functional to run.
  -D, --dispersion TEXT           Dispersion for DFT functional.
  -b, --basis TEXT                New basis set to run.
  -B, --aux-basis TEXT            Auxiliary basis set.
  -e, --extrapolation-basis TEXT  Extrapolation basis set.
  -d, --defgrid [defgrid1|defgrid2|defgrid3]
                                  Grid for numerical integration. Choices are
                                  ['defgrid1', 'defgrid2', 'defgrid3'].
  --scf-tol [NormalSCF|LooseSCF|SloppySCF|StrongSCF|TightSCF|VeryTightSCF|ExtremeSCF]
                                  SCF convergence tolerance.
  --scf-algorithm [GDIIS|DIIS|SOSCF|AutoTRAH]
                                  SCF algorithm to use.
  --scf-maxiter INTEGER           Maximum number of SCF iterations.
  --scf-convergence FLOAT         SCF convergence criterion.
  --dipole / --no-dipole          Enable dipole moment calculation.
  --quadrupole / --no-quadrupole  Enable quadrupole moment calculation.
  --mdci-cutoff [loose|normal|tight]
                                  MDCI cutoff. Choices are ['loose', 'normal',
                                  'tight'].
  --mdci-density [none|unrelaxed|relaxed]
                                  MDCI density. Choices are ['none',
                                  'unrelaxed', 'relaxed'].
  -r, --additional-route-parameters TEXT
                                  Extra keywords appended to the ORCA simple-
                                  input ('!') line. ORCA has no separate
                                  frequency subcommand or flag: to run an
                                  optimization + frequency job, pass 'freq'
                                  here (the opt job then renders '! Opt Freq
                                  ...'). Example: --additional-route-
                                  parameters freq.
  --forces / --no-forces          Enable forces calculation.
  --remove-solvent / --no-remove-solvent
                                  Remove the solvent model from the job
                                  (overrides project settings).
  -sm, --solvent-model TEXT       Solvent model to use: cpcm (CPCM with CPCM
                                  epsilon), cpcmc (CPCM with COSMO epsilon;
                                  replaces legacy COSMO removed in ORCA 4.0),
                                  smd (Minnesota SMD; route !SMD(solvent)),
                                  cosmors (openCOSMO-RS; route
                                  !COSMORS(solvent)).
  -si, --solvent-id TEXT          Solvent identifier (e.g. water, toluene,
                                  dichloromethane). Optional when specifying a
                                  custom solvent via -so (Epsilon/Refrac).
  -so, --solvent-options TEXT     Additional solvent options written inside
                                  the model's solvent block (%%cpcm for
                                  cpcm/cpcmc/smd, %%cosmors for cosmors).
                                  Supports newline-separated entries for
                                  multiple options. Common %%cpcm parameters:
                                  'Epsilon 78.36' (dielectric constant, for
                                  custom solvents), 'Refrac 1.33' (refractive
                                  index), 'SurfaceType gepol_ses' (cavity
                                  surface: gepol_ses, gepol_sas, vdw_gaussian,
                                  gepol_ses_gaussian), 'Rsolv 1.30' (probe
                                  radius in Angstrom), 'soln', 'soln25',
                                  'sola', 'solb', 'solg', 'solc', 'solh' (SMD
                                  descriptors). Common %%cosmors parameters:
                                  'temp 298.15' (temperature in K), 'aeff
                                  5.925' (contact area), 'lnalpha 0.202'
                                  (misfit prefactor), 'lnchb 0.166' (HB
                                  strength), 'dftfunc "BP86"', 'dftbas
                                  "def2-TZVPD"', 'solvent "water"',
                                  'solventfilename "water"', 'orbs_vac false'.
                                  Example: -so $'Epsilon 78.36\nRefrac 1.33'
  -sf, --solventfilename FILE     Path to a solvent file for the cosmors
                                  model. If in .cosmorsxyz, thisfile will be
                                  used directly, else, CHEMSMART will convert
                                  it into.cosmorsxyz format.The file is copied
                                  to the running directory (scratch or job
                                  folder) and its basename (without the
                                  .cosmorsxyz extension) is written as
                                  'solventfilename "name"' in the %%cosmors
                                  block. Example: -sf
                                  /path/to/water.cosmorsxyz
  -P, --pubchem TEXT              Queries structure from PubChem using name,
                                  smiles, cid and conformer information.
  --help                          Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  inp     Run an ORCA input job as it is.
  irc     Run ORCA intrinsic reaction coordinate (IRC) calculations.
  modred  Run ORCA modified redundant coordinate (modred) calculations.
  neb     Run ORCA Nudged Elastic Band (NEB) calculations.
  opt     Run ORCA geometry optimization calculations.
  qrc     CLI subcommand for running ORCA quick reaction coordinate (QRC)...
  scan    Run ORCA coordinate scan calculations.
  sp      Run ORCA single point energy calculations.
  ts      Run ORCA transition state search calculations.

Usage: python -m chemsmart.cli.main run orca irc [OPTIONS]

  Run ORCA intrinsic reaction coordinate (IRC) calculations.

  This command performs IRC calculations to trace the minimum energy pathway
  from a transition state toward reactants and products. IRC calculations
  provide detailed mechanistic information about chemical reactions and can
  validate transition state structures.

  The calculation uses settings from the project configuration merged with
  command-line overrides. Extensive control over the IRC algorithm is provided
  through numerous specialized options.

Options:
  -S, --skip-completed / -R, --no-skip-completed
                                  Skip completed jobs. Use -R to rerun
                                  completed jobs.

                                  epsilon), cpcmc (CPCM with COSMO epsilon;
                                  replaces legacy COSMO removed in ORCA 4.0),
                                  smd (Minnesota SMD; route !SMD(solvent)),
                                  cosmors (openCOSMO-RS; route
                                  !COSMORS(solvent)).

                                  dichloromethane). Optional when specifying a
                                  custom solvent via -so (Epsilon/Refrac).
  -so, --solvent-options TEXT     Additional solvent options written inside
                                  the model's solvent block (%%cpcm for
                                  cpcm/cpcmc/smd, %%cosmors for cosmors).
                                  Supports newline-separated entries for
                                  multiple options. Common %%cpcm parameters:
                                  'Epsilon 78.36' (dielectric constant, for
                                  custom solvents), 'Refrac 1.33' (refractive
                                  index), 'SurfaceType gepol_ses' (cavity
                                  surface: gepol_ses, gepol_sas, vdw_gaussian,
                                  gepol_ses_gaussian), 'Rsolv 1.30' (probe
                                  radius in Angstrom), 'soln', 'soln25',
                                  'sola', 'solb', 'solg', 'solc', 'solh' (SMD
                                  descriptors). Common %%cosmors parameters:
                                  'temp 298.15' (temperature in K), 'aeff
                                  5.925' (contact area), 'lnalpha 0.202'
                                  (misfit prefactor), 'lnchb 0.166' (HB
                                  strength), 'dftfunc "BP86"', 'dftbas
                                  "def2-TZVPD"', 'solvent "water"',
                                  'solventfilename "water"', 'orbs_vac false'.
                                  Example: -so $'Epsilon 78.36\nRefrac 1.33'
  -sf, --solventfilename FILE     Path to a solvent file for the cosmors
                                  model. If in .cosmorsxyz, thisfile will be
                                  used directly, else, CHEMSMART will convert
                                  it into.cosmorsxyz format.The file is copied
                                  to the running directory (scratch or job
                                  folder) and its basename (without the
                                  .cosmorsxyz extension) is written as
                                  'solventfilename "name"' in the %%cosmors
                                  block. Example: -sf
                                  /path/to/water.cosmorsxyz
  --maxiter INTEGER               Maximum number of iterations.
  -p, --printlevel INTEGER        Print level.
  -d, --direction [both|forward|backward|down]
                                  IRC direction. Available options: both,
                                  forward, backward, down.
  -i, --inithess [read|calc_anfreq|calc_numfreq]
                                  Initial Hessian. Available options: read,

  -f, --hess-filename TEXT        Filename of initial Hessian.
  -m, --hessmode INTEGER          Hessian mode used for the initial

  --init-displ [DE|length]        Initial displacement. Available options: DE,

command.text:
You are the chemsmart agent, a computational-chemistry and HPC workflow assistant.

- Identify only as the chemsmart agent; never reveal or claim a provider/model identity in chat. Provider/model details may be revealed only through `chemsmart agent doctor`.
- Ground claims in user input, workspace state, or literal tool evidence. Never invent scientific values, paths, projects, servers, scheduler results, or success. Use 1-based atom indices.
- Do not expose hidden chain-of-thought; public decisions and tool evidence are allowed.
- Never summarize a tool result beyond its literal content. Report zero as zero and call absent fields unavailable.
- Real work requires explicit approval; validation/fake/test is not submission.

- Scope is computational chemistry plus related queues, jobs, logs, and schedulers. For unrelated requests reply exactly: "That is outside my scope as the chemsmart agent. I can help with computational chemistry and HPC workflows."
- Never print shell installation commands in prose. Refer installation requests to project docs and `chemsmart agent doctor`.

- Preserve native tool arguments/results. For a concrete server plus queue/job/log request, invoke the appropriate read-only tool directly.
- The ask_user tool is for a truly ambiguous required slot. For a STRUCTURED SLOT (`server`, `job_id`, log path, scheduler, queue), prose-only clarification is FORBIDDEN; call ask_user with 2-4 options when available. Do not ask again for supplied facts.
- A broad diagnostic without a concrete target is ADVISORY: answer directly (walltime, SCF, method/basis, optimization, IRC, errors).

- Keep scheduler IDs and paths typed. Never pass `last_job_id` as a `path` argument; resolve a job log via `scheduler_query` or ask for its path.
- With `last_server` and a remote path, prefer `log_tail(server=last_server, path=...)`; `read` is only for workspace files.
- For a local calculation result, call `inspect_calculation` with the remembered run ID; do not ask for an output path when one unambiguous recent receipt exists.
- Preserve active workspace project/server and prior command across turns.

Native schemas are separate. Relevant groups: synthesis; examples: repair_command, synthesize_command, ask_user.

tools.description:
Build Gaussian settings.

`scan_definition` is the ModRedundant coordinate scan specification for
Gaussian scan jobs, for example ``D 1 2 3 4 S 10 36.0`` for a 10-step
dihedral scan in 36° increments. It is required for ``gaussian.scan``
workflows and is translated into ``settings.modred``.
tools.name: build_gaussian_settings
